headline: Target Stock Rises. An Earnings Beat Shows Its Turnaround Plan Is Working.
summary: The big-box retailer reports adjusted earnings of $1.71 a share, as revenue jumps 6.7% from a year ago.

headline: Target books strongest sales growth in 4 years with customers buying into refreshed line up
summary: Target, which embarked on a turnaround plan under its new CEO three months ago, reported its largest jump in comparable sales in four years Wednesday.  More customers buying in all six of Target’s main merchandising categories helped deliver the better-than-expected sales, the company said.  Comparable sales — those coming from stores and digital channels operating for at least 12 months, rose 5.6% in the three-month period ended May 2.

content.title: Target books strongest sales growth in 4 years with customers buying into refreshed line up
content.summary: Target, which embarked on a turnaround plan under its new CEO three months ago, reported its largest jump in comparable sales in four years Wednesday.  More customers buying in all six of Target’s main merchandising categories helped deliver the better-than-expected sales, the company said.  Comparable sales — those coming from stores and digital channels operating for at least 12 months, rose 5.6% in the three-month period ended May 2.

headline: Target (NYSE:TGT) Surprises With Strong Q1 CY2026
summary: General merchandise retailer Target (NYSE:TGT) reported revenue ahead of Wall Street’s expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 6.7% year on year to $25.44 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.71 per share was 17.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.
